tagline: Photonic interconnects that link quantum processors into unified distributed systems
founders: Dr. Mikhail Lukin, Dr. Hannes Bernien, Shankar Menon
what: CavilinQ builds cavity-enhanced photonic links that unify isolated quantum processors into single distributed systems, enabling utility-scale quantum computing through high-fidelity light-matter interfaces.
why: Quantum scaling has been bottlenecked by the inability to connect processors; CavilinQ's interconnect approach directly addresses this critical gap and could accelerate the quantum computing timeline by years.
signal: U Chicago & Harvard professor co-founders (Lukin, Bernien); University of Chicago Polsky Center spinout; led by QVT

tagline: Generative AI designs proximity-based drugs that control protein-protein interactions
founders: VantAI founding team
what: Proxima (formerly VantAI) uses generative AI foundation models trained on proteome-scale structural data to design proximity-based therapeutics that control protein-protein interactions for novel drug targets.
why: With partnerships at J&J, Bristol Myers Squibb, and Blueprint Medicines, and a first program entering clinical trials in 2026, Proxima validates AI-native drug discovery at commercial scale.
signal: $80M seed led by DCVC with NVentures (NVIDIA VC); multiple major pharma co-development partnerships; first clinical program advancing

tagline: Autonomous AI penetration testing that finds and validates vulnerabilities at superhuman speed
founders: Oege de Moor
what: XBOW is an autonomous offensive security platform that conducts penetration testing and vulnerability identification, solving 75% of web app security benchmarks and deployed in production at 100+ customers including Fortune 500 companies.
why: Founded by Oege de Moor—creator of GitHub Copilot and GitHub Advanced Security—XBOW brings unmatched credibility to a market hungry for automated pen testing at scale.
signal: Founder created GitHub Copilot & GitHub Advanced Security; Sequoia-backed seed; 100+ production customers; now valued at $1B+

tagline: Modular 'Legos for robots' platform lets teams deploy custom dextrous robots in days
founders: Mike Xia, Vijay Pradeep
what: Anvil Robotics builds a composable modular robot platform enabling businesses and AI teams to rapidly develop and deploy custom dextrous robots that cost $5K–$10K and arrive within 1–2 days.
why: As physical AI enters its deployment phase, hardware availability is the critical bottleneck; Anvil's democratized, fast-ship approach could become the AWS of robotics infrastructure.
signal: Led by Matter Venture Partners and Humba Ventures; Superhuman founder Vivek Sodera investing; manufacturing in Taiwan; SF-based 8-month-old startup

tagline: Solar-powered orbital data centers bring AI compute beyond Earth's constraints
founders: Starcloud team (YC alums)
what: Starcloud builds solar-powered data centers in low-Earth orbit using Nvidia H100 and Blackwell B200 GPUs, providing AI compute infrastructure unconstrained by terrestrial power and cooling limits.
why: The fastest YC company to reach unicorn status at 17 months, Starcloud has already launched a satellite with an H100 GPU and is rapidly scaling to dramatically more powerful orbital compute nodes.
signal: YC company; $1.1B valuation; led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures; first orbital GPU satellite launched Nov 2025; $200M total raised

tagline: AI voice agents conduct 40-minute research interviews autonomously at scale
founders: Miravoice founding team
what: Miravoice's AI voice agent platform autonomously conducts long-form phone surveys and interviews for market research—handling 120+ questions over 40+ minute calls—without any human interviewers.
why: By automating the most expensive, time-consuming part of qualitative research, Miravoice enables precision data collection at unprecedented scale, with a no-code platform that any researcher can deploy.
signal: Led by Unusual Ventures; angels from Ramp, PubMatic, Atlassian, and Google; announced April 2026

tagline: Stablecoin clearinghouse enables seamless, fee-free swaps between any dollar-backed token
founders: Sam Broner, Adam Zuckerman
what: The Better Money Company is a stablecoin clearinghouse that enables cheap, predictable, no-slippage exchange between different dollar-backed tokens, with integrations at Paxos, Bridge, MoonPay, MetaMask, and Phantom.
why: Stablecoin fragmentation is the last major friction point in the crypto payments stack; with a16z crypto leading and partnerships across the ecosystem, the timing is ideal as stablecoin regulation clarifies.
signal: Founded by Sam Broner (ex-a16z crypto investor) and Adam Zuckerman; led by a16z crypto with BoxGroup and Sunflower Capital

tagline: Autonomous AI agents log into ERPs and handle enterprise finance workflows end-to-end
founders: Zalos founding team
what: Zalos builds autonomous AI agents that log into enterprise ERPs and financial systems to automate repetitive finance processes—billing, reconciliations, accounts payable—with near-perfect accuracy required for financial-grade automation.
why: Finance automation demands accuracy far beyond typical AI benchmarks; Zalos's near-perfect accuracy bar and enterprise ERP integration unlock a massive market that generalist AI agents cannot serve.
signal: Backed by 14 Peaks; angels include FedEx CFO Mike Lenz, Indeed founder Paul Forster; London/SF-based; founded 2025

tagline: AI-native SEO agency combines autonomous agents with human experts for organic growth
founders: Thenuka Karunaratne
what: Daydream is an AI-native digital agency that combines proven SEO methodology with AI agents to automate organic search optimization, blending autonomous agents with human domain experts for high-growth companies.
why: As AI disrupts traditional SEM channels, SEO becomes a strategic moat—Daydream's hybrid human-AI model is positioned to capture the enterprise segment that pure AI tools cannot yet serve reliably.
signal: Series A led by WndrCo with First Round Capital and Basis Set Ventures; $21M total raised; YC ranked AI agencies #3 most-wanted startup category

tagline: Fintech infrastructure making venture secondary markets simpler and more accessible
founders: Earlyasset founding team
what: Earlyasset is building critical technology and financial infrastructure for venture secondary markets, enabling simpler and more accessible secondary transactions for venture-backed shareholders and founders with proprietary pricing transparency.
why: With 80% of secondary volume concentrated in just 10 companies and most founders locked out of liquidity, Earlyasset's infrastructure layer addresses a structurally broken market at exactly the right moment.
signal: Led by New Stack Ventures with Cervin Ventures; emerged from stealth April 2026; Park City, UT-based

tagline: Behavioral AI detects theft and suspicious activity in existing security cameras—no hardware needed
founders: David Elskamp, Liam Webster
what: Lexius embeds advanced AI into existing security camera systems to detect theft, slip-and-falls, and suspicious behavior using behavioral recognition that intentionally ignores personal characteristics to prevent bias.
why: Works with existing camera infrastructure (zero hardware capex), eliminates discriminatory surveillance, and already serves 7-Eleven, Erewhon, and Prada—demonstrating enterprise product-market fit.
signal: YC W26 (top batch); named one of TechCrunch's 16 most interesting W26 startups; enterprise clients include 7-Eleven, Erewhon, Prada

tagline: Merchant OS transforms African tier-1 banks into fintechs and SMEs into digital enterprises
founders: Brandon Roberts, Miod Davith Kahwa
what: Littlefish is a merchant operating system powering relationships between tier-1 African banks—Standard Bank, FNB, Absa—and the small business merchant class, turning banks into fintechs across 10+ African markets.
why: 30x revenue growth since seed, a Visa strategic partnership, and an underbanked SME market of hundreds of millions make Littlefish the infrastructure play for Africa's digital commerce layer.
signal: 30x revenue growth since seed; clients include Standard Bank, FNB, Absa; Visa strategic partnership; led by Partech with Proparco and TLcom Capital

tagline: Organelle engineering to future-proof global food crops
founders: University of Manchester spinout — plant cell biology & chloroplast engineering PhD scientists
what: Cytotrait's MOSS (Mutant Organelle Selection System) engineers traits inside plant organelles (chloroplasts, mitochondria) rather than the nuclear genome — enabling dramatically higher trait expression, simpler stacking, and a smoother regulatory pathway. Targets wheat, maize, potato, canola.
why: MOSS is a platform technology hitting four critical food crops at the moment climate stress is forcing regenerative agriculture onto the policy agenda. ARIA government grant validates the science pre-seed. £3M entry price before any clinical/field data.
signal: Northern Gritstone, UK Innovation & Science Seed Fund, ARIA grant

tagline: Smart, programmable mRNA medicines that activate only in disease cells
founders: Jim Collins (MIT Professor, one of the world's most cited bioengineers, co-inventor of synthetic gene circuits); Eerik Kaseniit (CSO); Sophia Lugo (CEO)
what: Radar builds mRNA therapeutics with proprietary RNA sensors that gate payload expression based on the specific molecular environment inside a target cell. Medicines only activate where specific RNA signatures are detected. Applicable across oncology, neurodegeneration, and immunology.
why: Jim Collins' lab has an extraordinary track record of commercialization. Eli Lilly's strategic participation at seed stage is a rare big-pharma early endorsement. First clinical programs entering in 2026.
signal: NfX Bio (lead), Eli Lilly (strategic), Biovision Ventures, KdT Ventures, PearVC

tagline: AI-designed regulatory DNA for safer cell & gene therapies
founders: Yash (CTO, UIUC CS, ex-Automorphic YC S23) and Malhar (CEO, UIUC CS) — ML research backgrounds; additional scientist from Carolyn Bertozzi's Nobel laureate lab at Stanford
what: Origin Bio built Axis, the first AI model that both generates regulatory DNA elements and predicts their function. Outperforms Google DeepMind's AlphaGenome at predicting regulatory element activity by 6.7%. Enables programmable gene expression switches for cell and gene therapies.
why: Cell and gene therapy is one of biopharma's hottest areas but off-target expression is a serious safety risk. Outperforming DeepMind's best model at seed stage is an exceptional technical signal. YC W26.
signal: Y Combinator W26

tagline: Evolutionary therapies for autoimmune disease, inspired by parasites
founders: Adair Borges, Dennis Sun, Emily Weiss — three PhDs trained at UCSF, UCSD, and UC Berkeley with 40+ combined years in computational biology, host-parasite interactions, and AI protein structure prediction
what: Ditto Bio discovers drugs for autoimmune diseases by mining the biochemical toolkit of parasites (ticks, worms, viruses) that have evolved over millions of years to suppress the very immune pathways that go haywire in autoimmune conditions. 1M+ proteins analyzed, thousands of candidates identified.
why: Autoimmune diseases are a $150B+ market with significant unmet need. The parasite-derived drug discovery angle is genuinely novel. Few teams globally have the combined wet-lab and computational depth to execute this. YC W26.
signal: Y Combinator W26

tagline: RNA foundation models — AlphaFold for RNA therapeutics
founders: Team of PhDs from University of Toronto; industry experience at Recursion and Deep Genomics; founding scientist from Carolyn Bertozzi's Nobel laureate lab at Stanford; 47+ scientific publications
what: Blank Bio trains foundation models on RNA biology to power drug design and clinical pipeline applications. Their first model achieves state-of-the-art mRNA property prediction and can simulate wet-lab experiments computationally. Already in production use at Sanofi and GSK.
why: RNA therapeutics have exploded post-COVID but drug design remains largely empirical. Foundation model adoption already proven at Sanofi/GSK validates both the science and commercial readiness at an extremely early stage. YC S25.
signal: Y Combinator S25; Sanofi and GSK production users

tagline: AI-native biotech for proximity-based therapeutics — targeting undruggable proteins
founders: AI drug-discovery team (formerly VantAI)
what: Proxima builds the data and AI layer to unlock proximity therapeutics — drugs that work by forcing specific proteins into close proximity. NeoLink maps protein-protein interactions at proteome-wide scale; Neo AI model series turns that into drug candidates. Targets previously undruggable proteins. First programs entering the clinic in 2026.
why: Proximity therapeutics (PROTACs, molecular glues) are viewed as one of the next great drug classes. NVIDIA's NVentures strategic backing signals serious AI infrastructure validation. Real clinical timelines in 2026.
signal: DCVC (lead), NVentures (NVIDIA), Braidwell, Roivant, Alexandria Venture Investments

tagline: Kit-based automated liquid biopsy for lung cancer — no central lab required
founders: Sean Macfarlane (CEO); Belfast-based team
what: Biomarx builds a kit-based automated genomic diagnostic platform for next-generation sequencing cancer detection. First test targets non-small cell lung cancer using circulating tumour DNA from a blood draw, cutting turnaround from 3+ weeks to days at a fraction of centralized-lab cost.
why: Liquid biopsy is on track to replace tissue biopsy for many cancers, but complexity and cost keep them locked in centralized labs. Biomarx's kit-based approach could democratize access in local hospital labs. Extremely early and underpriced.
signal: SFC Capital, Clarendon Fund Managers, University of Strathclyde; winner InterTradeIreland Seedcorn Competition

tagline: World models to fix AI's fundamental architecture — Yann LeCun's bet
founders: Yann LeCun (Turing Award winner, 'godfather of deep learning', former Meta VP & Chief AI Scientist) as Executive Chair; Alexandre LeBrun (CEO); Saining Xie (CSO); Pascale Fung; Mike Rabbat
what: AMI Labs is building world models — AI systems that reason about physical reality and causality grounded in the physical world rather than predicting the next text token. LeCun's bet is that JEPA (joint embedding predictive architectures) are the right path to genuine reasoning, not transformers trained on internet text.
why: One of the field's founding scientists publicly disagreeing with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google and putting $1B behind an alternative. Whether right or wrong, AMI will generate pivotal research and draw top global talent. Europe's largest seed round ever.
signal: Cathay Innovation, Greycroft, Hiro Capital, HV Capital, Bezos Expeditions, NVIDIA, Temasek

tagline: The trust and governance OS for enterprise AI agents
founders: Sri Viswanath — former CTO at Atlassian, former Coatue partner; 20+ years at Sun Microsystems, VMware
what: Sycamore is building the operating system for enterprise AI agents. Agents start heavily monitored and earn autonomy incrementally as they prove reliability — a tiered trust model handling deployment, security, identity, and orchestration across heterogeneous agent ecosystems.
why: Every enterprise is wrestling with how to operationalize AI agents safely. The angel list is exceptional for enterprise GTM signal — Bob McGrew (ex-OpenAI Chief Scientist), Databricks CEO, Intel CEO, Okta co-founder.
signal: Coatue & Lightspeed (co-lead); angels: Bob McGrew, Ali Ghodsi, Lip-Bu Tan, Frederic Kerrest, Mike Knoop

tagline: Modular quantum computing interconnects — the missing scaling layer
founders: Shankar Menon (CEO, PhD '25 UChicago); scientific co-founders Hannes Bernien (UChicago) and Mikhail Lukin (Harvard — arguably the most decorated quantum physicist in the US, National Academy of Sciences fellow)
what: CavilinQ builds quantum interconnects — hardware that physically networks separate quantum processors together, the same way servers are networked in classical data centers. Using high-fidelity light-matter interfaces, they address the scaling problem that prevents current quantum computers from reaching utility-scale.
why: Quantum interconnects are the consensus 'hardest missing piece' for utility-scale quantum computing. Mikhail Lukin as co-founder is one of the strongest academic signals possible in the quantum space. Announced April 2 — barely discovered.
signal: QVT Ventures (lead), Safar Partners, MFV Partners, Serendipity Capital; UChicago Polsky Commercialization Fellow

tagline: Composable hardware platform for physical AI — Stripe for robot infrastructure
founders: San Francisco-based team; hardware shipping velocity and real revenue at 8 months old
what: Anvil builds a composable stack of hardware modules, software, and data tools that physical AI teams can assemble to build custom robots — without reinventing controllers, teleoperation, and data pipelines from scratch. Ships 100+ units to 50+ customers in 1-2 days via air freight from Taiwan.
why: Picks-and-shovels play for the physical AI wave. 50+ paying customers, $1M+ ARR, 100+ units shipped at 8 months old — extraordinary hardware company traction. Announced April 2-3, largely undiscovered.
signal: Matter Venture Partners & Humba Ventures (co-lead), DNX Ventures, Spacecadet, Position Ventures

tagline: Open-source AI coding agent — GitLab founder's next bet
founders: Sid Sijbrandij (GitLab co-founder & executive chair, built GitLab to $15B) and Scott Breitenother (founder of Brooklyn Data)
what: Model-agnostic, open-source AI coding agent supporting 500+ models, running in VS Code, JetBrains, CLI, and cloud. Since early 2025: 750,000 downloads, #1 on OpenRouter, 6 trillion tokens/month processed.
why: Sid Sijbrandij is applying the same open-source developer infrastructure playbook that built GitLab into a $15B company. General Catalyst participation alongside those traction numbers is a strong institutional signal.
signal: Cota Capital (lead), General Catalyst

tagline: AI voice agents that run long-form phone surveys at scale
founders: Core team with enterprise SaaS and voice AI backgrounds
what: Miravoice deploys AI voice agents that autonomously conduct quantitative phone surveys — 120+ questions, 40+ minutes per call — at scale across millions of simultaneous calls. Replacing human interviewers in market research and polling with AI at a fraction of the cost.
why: Going after the outbound quantitative research market — a multi-billion dollar industry not yet disrupted. Unusual Ventures lead is a high-quality signal. Announced April 2.
signal: Unusual Ventures (lead), Neo, 25madison; angels from Ramp, Atlassian, PubMatic, Google

tagline: The context layer that makes AI agents understand people
founders: Michael Fanous (CEO, UC Berkeley CS, former ML engineer at CareRev) and Emad Fanous (CTO) — a father-son team
what: AI agents can't reliably link a person's LinkedIn, Instagram, public records, and app activity as belonging to the same individual. Nyne deploys millions of micro-agents to analyze public digital footprints across major and niche platforms (Strava, SoundCloud) to build a unified human context profile that other AI agents can query.
why: The 'context problem' is one of the sleeper issues in the agent wave. Gil Elbaz built the semantic infrastructure Google used for AdSense — his backing is a pointed endorsement of the data-layer thesis.
signal: Wischoff Ventures & South Park Commons (co-lead); angel: Gil Elbaz (co-founder Applied Semantics, pioneer of Google AdSense)

tagline: AI that hacks before attackers do — $1M ARR in 8 weeks
founders: Huzaifa Ahmad (UC Berkeley CS '25, ex-AWS, ex-PlayAI acquired by Meta), Ahmad Khan, Prama Yudhistira
what: Hex Security runs AI agents that continuously and autonomously penetration-test apps and infrastructure — replacing the once-a-year manual pen test model with 24/7 adversarial probing. Found critical SQL injection vulnerabilities, demonstrated network worms, accessed hundreds of codebases.
why: Pen testing is a multi-billion dollar market still largely run by humans. $1M ARR in 8 weeks from Demo Day — among the fastest in YC history. Product-market fit established before most VCs have met the founders. YC W26.
signal: Y Combinator W26; $1M+ ARR in 8 weeks

tagline: Claude Code for science — AI co-scientists for end-to-end research
founders: Aayam Bansal (18, Harvard CS) and Ishaan Gangwani (MIT CS) — among the youngest founders in YC W26 history
what: A platform where scientists orchestrate AI co-scientists that handle the full research loop: literature review → hypothesis generation → experiment design → results analysis → paper drafts. Applies the coding agent model to scientific research execution.
why: Raised $1.4M before even starting YC — exceptionally strong pre-YC investor conviction. At $1.9M total, this is as early-stage as it gets. Founded by 18-year-olds without assumptions baked in. YC W26.
signal: Y Combinator W26; $1.4M raised pre-YC

tagline: AI device that records, transcribes, and summarizes conversations — offline, in real time
founders: Akshay Narisetti (Georgia Tech, robotics builder since age 12, 100+ robots built); Gabriel Dymowski (ex-CEO DoxyChain)
what: Wearable AI hardware that captures and summarizes meetings and conversations on-device with no cloud dependency
why: YC W26 revenue outlier: $27M ARR with 50% MoM growth and 30K+ units shipped at $129–$327 price point. Rare hardware+AI combination with strong real-world traction.
signal: YC W26 top revenue performer; 472 reviews at 4.89 stars; no cloud required (privacy moat)

tagline: Building world models for real-world AI — beyond next-token prediction
founders: Yann LeCun (Turing Award winner, inventor of CNNs, Meta VP of AI Research, NYU Professor)
what: Developing Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) world models for AI systems that can reason about and navigate the physical world
why: The godfather of deep learning bets his legacy on JEPA as the path beyond LLMs. If world models are the next frontier, no one is better positioned. Europe's largest-ever seed round at $1.03B validates the conviction.
signal: Co-led by Cathay Innovation, Greycroft, HV Capital; backed by Eric Schmidt, Mark Cuban, Bezos Expeditions, Tim Berners-Lee

tagline: Next-generation AI systems built by the team that shipped GPT-4 and ChatGPT
founders: Mira Murati (ex-CTO OpenAI, led GPT-4/ChatGPT/DALL-E launches); 30+ researchers from OpenAI, Meta AI, Mistral
what: Building advanced AI systems for real-world deployment — details still emerging, but team pedigree is unmatched
why: Largest seed round in history at $2B. Murati ran the product and engineering org that created the most impactful AI products of the decade. March 2026 Nvidia partnership ($1 gigawatt Vera Rubin compute) signals frontier ambition.
signal: Led by a16z; Nvidia, AMD, Cisco, Jane Street as strategic investors; Nvidia $1GW compute partnership

tagline: Agentic AI drug company running the full discovery pipeline on biological foundation models
founders: David van Dijk, PhD (Yale Professor, 11,000+ citations, published in Cell/Nature/ICML/NeurIPS, turned down Google offer); Ivan Vrkic (built core tech at Yale, ICML publications, large-scale foundation model training)
what: AI agents autonomously run the entire drug discovery pipeline using biological foundation models trained to simulate human biology
why: YC W26 with top-tier academic founder (Yale Prof with 11K citations), co-developed Cell2Sentence with Google DeepMind, and already discovered a validated cancer treatment signal with top-10 pharma clients live.
signal: YC W26; co-developed Cell2Sentence with Google DeepMind; active top-10 pharma partnerships

tagline: Blood-brain-barrier-penetrating antibody targeting amyloid-beta in Alzheimer's disease
founders: Jonathan Violin, PhD (serial biotech founder; founding CEO of Viridian, Dianthus, Quellis; Fairmount venture partner)
what: Novel BBB-crossing antibody (KRSA-028) targeting amyloid-beta plaques in Alzheimer's, aiming for clinical debut in 2027
why: Alzheimer's remains one of medicine's largest unmet needs. Novel BBB-penetration mechanism differentiates from existing amyloid antibodies. $175M total raised with blue-chip syndicate.
signal: Led by Wellington Management and TCGX; J.P. Morgan Life Sciences, Sanofi Ventures, Foresite Capital as co-investors

tagline: Long-term memory systems for physical AI — making robots that remember
founders: University of Oxford spinout team
what: Building persistent memory and state-tracking infrastructure for embodied AI robots so they can learn and retain context across interactions
why: Long-term memory is the missing layer for practical robotics deployment. Oxford spinout with deep academic IP, backed by Amadeus Capital Partners and Oxford Science Enterprises.
signal: Oxford University spinout; led by Amadeus Capital Partners and Oxford Science Enterprises; March 2026

tagline: Ultra-low latency multilingual voice AI — making voice the universal AI interface
founders: Neil Zeghidour, CEO (Kyutai founding member, ex-Google DeepMind voice AI researcher); Laurent Mazare & Alexandre Defossez (Kyutai scientists); Olivier Teboul (ex-Google engineer)
what: Real-time multilingual voice AI platform with sub-100ms latency for natural, fluid voice interactions across languages
why: Europe's largest AI voice seed at $70M. DeepMind voice AI pedigree, 12 paying enterprise customers within 3 months of launch. Voice interfaces are the next UX frontier.
signal: Led by FirstMark Capital, Eurazeo; Eric Schmidt, Xavier Niel, DST Global as investors; 12 enterprise customers in 3 months

tagline: AI-powered molecular matchmaker designing proximity-based therapeutics
founders: Expert biotech team (formerly VantAI) focused on protein-protein interaction therapeutics
what: AI platform for designing proximity-inducing drugs (molecular glues, PROTACs) that bring target proteins together — a novel modality beyond traditional small molecules
why: Proximity-based therapeutics are one of the hottest new drug modalities. AI-native approach with J&J and BMS partnerships and first program entering clinic in 2026.
signal: Led by DCVC; Nvidia NVentures, Roivant, Blueprint Medicines as strategic investors; J&J and Bristol Myers Squibb partnerships

tagline: Auditable AI agents for enterprise finance — CFO-grade accuracy with full transparency
founders: Parijat Sarkar & Mitul Tiwari
what: AI finance platform with transparent, auditable agents handling reconciliations, variance analysis, and financial reporting for enterprise CFOs
why: Enterprise finance AI is a massive market. Numos's focus on auditability is the right wedge. Led by General Catalyst with live customers (Dandy, Udemy) and 80% faster planning cycles. Announced April 3, 2026.
signal: Led by General Catalyst, Operator Collective; live enterprise customers including Dandy and Udemy

tagline: AI-first drug discovery for immunology — Nobel Prize-winning protein design at scale
founders: David Baker, PhD (UW Professor, Director of Institute for Protein Design, Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024); Hetu Kamisetty, PhD (ex-Meta/IPD); Arvind Rajpal, PhD (ex-Genentech)
what: AI-native R&D platform for inflammatory/immunological diseases using ML models (X-Cell) trained on cellular perturbation data for antibody therapy design
why: Nobel Prize-winning founder (Baker, Chemistry 2024) is the world's foremost protein design scientist. $1B committed seed with Sequoia, Arch, Foresite backing is extraordinarily rare.
signal: Incubated by Arch Venture Partners & Foresite Labs; investors include Sequoia, NEA, Lux Capital, Lightspeed, F-Prime

tagline: Cancer moonshot targeting p53 suppression in 50% of human tumors via PARN enzyme inhibition
founders: Roy Parker, PhD (CU Boulder Professor, RNA biology expert, co-founder)
what: Novel cancer therapy targeting the PARN enzyme to restore p53 tumor suppressor function — applicable to roughly 50% of all human tumors including colorectal, triple-negative breast, and pancreatic cancers
why: P53 is mutated or suppressed in half of all cancers. If PARN inhibition can broadly restore p53 function, this is a platform therapy with enormous reach. University spinout with validated academic mechanism.
signal: CU Boulder pre-seed spinout program; PI-founded with strong academic IP; targeting large unmet oncology markets
